produced_by: worker-0 — Platform Identity + Governance + Matching Rules v2 deep audit

platform_identity.name: CrisPRO
platform_identity.tagline: Mechanism-Alignment Engine for Clinical Trial Stratification
platform_identity.what_crispro_is: CrisPRO is a computational platform that encodes patient biology and therapeutic intervention mechanisms in a shared 8-dimensional biological vector space, computes quantitative alignment between patient and trial, and produces ranked trial recommendations with mechanistic rationale. It is NOT a clinical decision support tool, NOT a diagnostic, and NOT a drug discovery platform.

layer_1_mechanism_fit.name: Mechanism Fit Engine (PATH A)
layer_1_mechanism_fit.what_it_does: Encodes patient biology as an 8D vector (p) and trial mechanism as an 8D vector (t). Computes fit = clip((p·t)/‖t‖₂, 0, 1). Outputs a fit score [0,1] with axis-level contribution breakdown.
layer_1_mechanism_fit.formula_status: PATH A — LOCKED 2026-04-28 by Fahad Kiani
layer_1_mechanism_fit.path_b_status: PROHIBITED — cosine similarity with ‖p‖₂ in denominator

layer_2_holistic_score.name: Holistic Score Engine
layer_2_holistic_score.what_it_does: Combines mechanism fit with eligibility, pharmacogenomic safety, and resistance risk into a single holistic score.

8d_framework.ceiling_explanation: The practical ceiling of 0.8898 arises from the ‖p_ref‖₂ constraint — a patient vector with all axes at 1.0 would score 1.0, but real patient vectors have heterogeneous axis values. The reference patient vector ‖p_ref‖₂ ≈ 0.8898 sets the practical maximum.

what_crispro_never_does: Does NOT predict individual patient outcomes
Does NOT generate novel drug candidates
Does NOT claim FDA-cleared diagnostic status
Does NOT use PATH B formula (cosine similarity with ‖p‖₂ in denominator) — PROHIBITED
Does NOT cite LATIFY delta values (QUARANTINED — version conflict unresolved)
Does NOT claim GBM ZEB1 as an escape marker (PERMANENTLY CLOSED)
Does NOT claim BRIEF-2 (PTEN-null→ITGAV) is CRISPR-validated (COMPUTATIONAL HYPOTHESIS)

CAP_01.name: 8D Patient Vector Construction
CAP_01.what_it_does: Constructs an 8-dimensional patient biology vector from molecular profiling data (NGS, IHC, ctDNA, imaging)
inputs: NGS panel (KRAS, NRAS, BRAF, ATM, BRCA1/2, PTEN, PIK3CA, CCNE1)
CAP_01.output: 8D patient vector p = [ddr, mapk, pi3k, vegf, her2, io, efflux, rss] ∈ [0,1]⁸
CAP_01.cannot_do: Cannot construct patient vector without molecular profiling data. Cannot infer axis values from clinical features alone.

CAP_02.name: Mechanism Fit Engine (PATH A)
CAP_02.what_it_does: Computes fit = clip((p·t)/‖t‖₂, 0, 1) between patient vector p and trial vector t. Returns fit score, axis contributions, and fit label.
CAP_02.cannot_do: Cannot compute fit without both patient vector and trial vector. PATH B formula is PROHIBITED.

CAP_03.name: Trial Vector Library
CAP_03.what_it_does: Maintains a curated library of 8D trial mechanism vectors for all programs in the CrisPRO corpus (22+ trials)
CAP_03.cannot_do: Cannot generate trial vectors for trials not in the corpus without manual curation.

CAP_04.name: Target-Lock (Metastasis Driver Identification)
CAP_04.what_it_does: Identifies genuine metastasis drivers using a composite algorithm: Evo2-7B (evolutionary constraint) + Enformer (chromatin accessibility) + AlphaFold 3 (structural confidence). Composite threshold ≥0.35 = genuine driver.

algorithm.evo2_7b: Evolutionary constraint — likelihood of WT vs mutant under evolutionary selection. High constraint = gene is under purifying selection = likely essential.
algorithm.enformer: Chromatin accessibility and gene regulatory activity — predicts ATAC-seq/ChIP-seq from DNA sequence. High activity = gene is actively transcribed in the tumor.
algorithm.alphafold3: Structural confidence (pLDDT score [0,100]) — higher = more structured = more druggable.

validated_on: OV ZEB1: HR=1.4803 (continuous Cox, bevacizumab arm OS)
CAP_04.cannot_do: CEACAM5 is NOT in the 38-gene panel — Target-Lock run not executed for CEACAM5. GBM ZEB1 is PERMANENTLY CLOSED.

CAP_05.name: SL Therapy Bridge v3
CAP_05.what_it_does: Maps patient SL vulnerabilities to actionable drugs with ALLOW/WARN/DISALLOW policy overlay. 20/20 tests PASS.
CAP_05.cannot_do: TP53 and genes not in SL_THERAPY_MAP return no sl_indicated_drugs. BRIEF-2 (PTEN-null→ITGAV) is NOT in SL_THERAPY_MAP.

CAP_06.name: BRIEF Program Asset Generation
CAP_06.what_it_does: Identifies synthetic lethality pairs from CRISPR functional genomics (DepMap), computes delta scores, applies FDR correction, and validates with targeted CRISPR experiments.
CAP_06.cannot_do: BRIEF-2 (PTEN-null→ITGAV) is COMPUTATIONAL HYPOTHESIS — DepMap NULL. Must disclose in every context.

CAP_07.name: EscapeMap (Bevacizumab Biomarker Stratification)
CAP_07.what_it_does: Characterizes ZEB1 as a prognostic biomarker in the bevacizumab arm using Cox regression, median-split, and tertile analyses.
validated_on: M2 stratum calibration: GEO 74.0% vs published 73.9%
CAP_07.cannot_do: GBM ZEB1 is PERMANENTLY CLOSED — 3 datasets all null/contradicted. Arm interaction p=0.246 — ZEB1 is PROGNOSTIC, NOT ARM-SPECIFIC.

CAP_08.name: Two-Gate Patient Selection Framework
CAP_08.what_it_does: Implements a two-gate patient selection framework for CEACAM5-targeted therapies: Gate 1 = IHC ≥80% (~28% of MSS mCRC); Gate 2 = IO permissiveness (pTMB ≥28 OR Immunoscore-IC high OR CD8+ TIL density high).
validated_on: CARMEN-LC03: IHC ≥80% OS HR=0.71 (post-hoc) vs IHC 50–79% PFS HR=1.38 (harmful)
CAP_08.cannot_do: Gate 1 fraction (28%) is OPEN_ASSUMPTION OA-08 — not validated in prospective data.

CAP_09.name: Vector-Scalpel Biomarker Ranking
CAP_09.what_it_does: Ranks biomarkers by their discriminative power for patient selection using the Vector-Scalpel algorithm.
validated_on: CEACAM5 ranking: cCEA (27/33) > pTMB (23/33) > IHC (20/33) > Immunoscore (19/33) > DetermaIO (15/33)
CAP_09.cannot_do: Ranking is specific to the CEACAM5 program — not generalizable to other programs without re-running.

CAP_10.name: Holistic Score Engine
CAP_10.what_it_does: Combines mechanism fit (40%), eligibility (30%), pharmacogenomic safety (15%), and resistance risk (15%) into a single holistic score.
CAP_10.cannot_do: Cannot compute holistic score without all four component scores.

formula_governance.path_b_status: PROHIBITED — cosine similarity with ‖p‖₂ in denominator
formula_governance.path_b_prohibition: PATH B is prohibited in ALL outputs. Any output using ‖p‖₂ in the denominator is invalid.

DL_07.reason: DDR axis value 0.983 is implausibly high — likely a computation error. Cannot be published until resolved.
DL_07.action_required: Recompute DDR axis value from source data

CT_03_DL_05_PC_06.reason: Patent cites delta +0.366; receipt cites delta +0.2641. Vector version unresolved.
CT_03_DL_05_PC_06.action_required: Resolve vector version conflict before citing any LATIFY delta value
CT_03_DL_05_PC_06.safe_to_cite: Responder score 0.8936 vs non-responder 0.6295 — CORRECT CITATION

PC_02.reason: Script does not reproduce documented deltas cleanly
PC_02.action_required: Fix retroactive_prediction_run.py before any claim about retroactive prediction accuracy

SC_001.conflict: CT26 carries BRAF V600E (ATCC/CCLE/COSMIC). BreAK CRC-001 Exclusion Criterion #7 requires BRAF wild-type patients.
SC_001.status: ACTIVE CONFLICT — must be resolved before enrollment
SC_001.action_required: Brenus Pharma must address CT26 BRAF V600E in the STC-1010 IND

GBM_ZEB1.reason: Three datasets all null or contradicted: GLASS (delta=−0.34, p=0.000184), GSE222515 (delta=−0.431, p=0.0334), GSE174554 snRNA-seq (delta=−0.076, p=0.742)
GBM_ZEB1.operational_meaning: Do not apply EscapeMap to GBM. Do not reopen without new evidence.

mandatory_disclosures: BRIEF-2 (PTEN-null→ITGAV) is a COMPUTATIONAL HYPOTHESIS — DepMap NULL across all 3 proxies — not CRISPR-validated. Must disclose in every context.
BRIEF-3 correlation r=0.050, p=0.780 is statistically insignificant. BRIEF-3 is MECHANISTICALLY_GROUNDED, not CRISPR_VALIDATED.
LATIFY delta values are QUARANTINED — do not cite any specific delta value.
GBM ZEB1 is PERMANENTLY CLOSED — do not claim ZEB1 as a GBM escape marker.
OV ZEB1 arm interaction p=0.246 — ZEB1 is PROGNOSTIC, NOT ARM-SPECIFIC.
Gate 1 fraction (28%) is OPEN_ASSUMPTION OA-08 — not validated in prospective data.
All deal figures are benchmarks from comparable transactions — not guaranteed outcomes.
CrisPRO does NOT predict individual patient outcomes and is NOT a clinical decision support tool.
prohibited_claims: GBM ZEB1 is an escape marker

matching_rules_v2.action: ACTIVATE program_BREAK_CRC_001 + program_IO_CORE + program_CEACAM5
matching_rules_v2.rationale: MSS/pMMR CRC is the primary indication for BreAK CRC-001 and the IO Core corpus. CEACAM5 is expressed in 98.7% of CRC.

matching_rules_v2.action: ACTIVATE program_CEACAM5; REQUIRE Gate 1 (IHC ≥80%) + Gate 2 (IO permissiveness)
matching_rules_v2.rationale: CEACAM5 IHC ≥80% is the validated selection threshold. IHC 50–79% is HARMFUL (PFS HR=1.38).

matching_rules_v2.action: ACTIVATE program_ATR_DDR; REQUIRE RS score + PTEN status
matching_rules_v2.rationale: ATR/WEE1/PARP inhibitors require DDR biomarker selection. RS-High ORR 40% vs RS-Low 5% (berzosertib).

matching_rules_v2.action: FLAG GAP-01; REQUIRE liver metastasis stratification; CITE CO.26 Pint=0.02
matching_rules_v2.rationale: Liver metastasis is the single most important IO stratification variable in MSS CRC. CO.26 liver-met PFS HR=1.39 (HARMFUL) vs no-LM HR=0.54.

matching_rules_v2.action: CITE CO.26 pTMB ≥28 OS HR=0.34; WARN tissue TMB is NOT a substitute (r=0.13, p=0.20)
matching_rules_v2.rationale: pTMB ≥28 is the strongest IO enrichment signal in MSS CRC from randomized data. Tissue TMB has poor correlation with pTMB in CRC.

matching_rules_v2.trigger: mechanism_keywords contains 'checkpoint' OR 'PD-1' OR 'PD-L1' AND indication contains 'MSS'
matching_rules_v2.action: ACTIVATE program_IO_CORE; CITE three conditions for IO benefit; WARN pharmacodynamic dissociation risk
matching_rules_v2.rationale: IO in MSS CRC requires all three conditions: NLM + pTMB ≥28 + inflamed TME. Unselected MSS CRC has <5% ORR to checkpoint inhibitors.

matching_rules_v2.action: CITE IMblaze370 failure (OS null); WARN cobimetinib suppresses T cell proliferation
matching_rules_v2.rationale: MEK inhibition in MSS CRC failed in Phase III (IMblaze370). Cobimetinib is antagonistic to IO.
matching_rules_v2.evidence: IMblaze370; IO failure archetype: MEK inhibition failure

matching_rules_v2.trigger: mechanism_keywords contains 'BRCA' OR 'PARP' AND failure_mode contains 'post-PARPi'
matching_rules_v2.action: ACTIVATE program_ATR_DDR CAPRI; CITE BRCA reversion + ABCB1 + RAD51 restoration resistance mechanisms
matching_rules_v2.rationale: Post-PARPi resistance has three mechanisms: BRCA reversion, ABCB1 upregulation, RAD51 restoration. CAPRI ORR 4% post-PARPi vs 36% naive.

matching_rules_v2.action: ACTIVATE CAP_07_EscapeMap; CITE HR=1.4803 (continuous Cox); WARN arm interaction p=0.246 (PROGNOSTIC NOT ARM-SPECIFIC)
matching_rules_v2.rationale: ZEB1 is a validated prognostic marker in the bevacizumab arm of ovarian cancer. Arm interaction is not significant.

matching_rules_v2.trigger: mechanism_keywords contains 'ZEB1' AND indication contains 'GBM' OR 'glioblastoma'
matching_rules_v2.action: BLOCK — GBM ZEB1 is PERMANENTLY CLOSED; DO NOT ACTIVATE EscapeMap for GBM
matching_rules_v2.rationale: Three datasets all null or contradicted. ZEB1 DECREASES in recurrent GBM — opposite of escape hypothesis.

matching_rules_v2.action: ACTIVATE program_BREAK_CRC_001; CITE QUILT-2.004 design precedent (HR 1.061); REQUIRE three conditions for IO benefit
matching_rules_v2.rationale: Whole-cell vaccine mechanisms in MSS CRC require IO permissiveness. QUILT-2.004 failed in unselected MSS CRC.

matching_rules_v2.trigger: mechanism_keywords contains 'bevacizumab' OR 'VEGF' AND indication contains 'CRC'
matching_rules_v2.action: CITE TRIBE2 canonical benchmark (mPFS 9.8 mo, ORR 50%, mOS 22.5 mo); WARN BECOME inflates mOS (liver-limited selection)
matching_rules_v2.rationale: TRIBE2 is the canonical comparator for mFOLFOX6 + bev in 1L mCRC. BECOME is liver-limited and inflates mOS.

matching_rules_v2.action: ACTIVATE program_ATR_DDR Adavosertib; WARN PTEN-loss PFS HR=1.82 (HARMFUL); REQUIRE PTEN status
matching_rules_v2.rationale: PTEN-loss creates CDK2 dependency that PROTECTS against WEE1i. PTEN-intact ORR 23% vs PTEN-loss ORR 0%.

matching_rules_v2.action: ACTIVATE CAP_06_BRIEF; CITE BRIEF-1 (CRISPR_VALIDATED, deal_ready=true); DISCLOSE BRIEF-2 (COMPUTATIONAL_HYPOTHESIS)
matching_rules_v2.rationale: BRIEF-1 and BRIEF-4 are CRISPR-validated SL assets. BRIEF-2 is a computational hypothesis — mandatory disclosure.

matching_rules_v2.trigger: mechanism_keywords contains 'Immunoscore' OR 'CD8' AND indication contains 'CRC'
matching_rules_v2.action: CITE AtezoTRIBE Immunoscore-IC high HR ~0.50 (exploratory); ACTIVATE MOD-05
matching_rules_v2.rationale: Immunoscore-IC is the best validated TME profiling tool in CRC. High Immunoscore-IC correlates with IO benefit.

matching_rules_v2.action: ACTIVATE program_IO_CORE REGONIVO; CITE US ORR 7% (5/70); CITE Japan ORR 33% (all 8 responders NLM)
matching_rules_v2.rationale: REGONIVO US failed in unselected MSS CRC. Japan success was entirely in NLM patients — confirms liver metastasis as the key stratification variable.

matching_rules_v2.action: ACTIVATE program_ATR_DDR Berzosertib; CITE RS score algorithm (CCNE1 amp + CDK2 + RRM2 + CDC45 + CLASPIN)
matching_rules_v2.rationale: RS-High patients have CCNE1 amplification and CDK2 overactivity — they are dependent on ATR for replication stress management.

matching_rules_v2.action: ACTIVATE program_ATR_DDR LATIFY; CITE responder 0.8936 vs non-responder 0.6295; DO NOT CITE delta (QUARANTINED)
matching_rules_v2.rationale: LATIFY uses STK11/KEAP1 gate for patient selection. Delta values are QUARANTINED — only cite responder/non-responder scores.

matching_rules_v2.action: ACTIVATE MOD-15 (Stromal Barrier); CITE immune exclusion domain; WARN pharmacodynamic dissociation risk
matching_rules_v2.rationale: TGF-β and CAFs create the stromal barrier that excludes T cells from the tumor center. This is the dominant failure mode in immune-excluded MSS CRC.

matching_rules_v2.action: CITE GVAX ORR 0% (0/22 despite confirmed T-cell responses); WARN pharmacodynamic dissociation
matching_rules_v2.rationale: GVAX confirmed T-cell priming but zero tumor responses — the canonical pharmacodynamic dissociation example in MSS CRC.

matching_rules_v2.trigger: mechanism_keywords contains 'neoantigen' OR 'tumor mutational burden' AND indication contains 'MSS'
matching_rules_v2.action: REQUIRE pTMB (GuardantOMNI) — tissue TMB is NOT a substitute; CITE r=0.13, p=0.20
matching_rules_v2.rationale: Tissue TMB has poor correlation with pTMB in CRC. pTMB ≥28 is the validated threshold.

matching_rules_v2.action: WARN IHC 50–79% is HARMFUL (PFS HR=1.38); REQUIRE IHC ≥80% for Gate 1
matching_rules_v2.rationale: CARMEN-LC03 showed IHC 50–79% is numerically harmful. Only IHC ≥80% showed directional OS benefit.

matching_rules_v2.action: BLOCK — BRIEF-2 (PTEN-null→ITGAV) is COMPUTATIONAL HYPOTHESIS; DISCLOSE DepMap NULL; DO NOT RECOMMEND as SL therapy
matching_rules_v2.rationale: BRIEF-2 DepMap NULL across all 3 proxies. Not CRISPR-validated. Mandatory disclosure.

matching_rules_v2.action: CITE responder 0.8936 vs non-responder 0.6295; DO NOT CITE delta (QUARANTINED); CITE STK11/KEAP1 gate
matching_rules_v2.rationale: LATIFY delta values are QUARANTINED — version conflict between patent (+0.366) and receipt (+0.2641) unresolved.

matching_rules_v2.action: FLAG SC-001 ACTIVE CONFLICT — CT26 carries BRAF V600E; BreAK CRC-001 requires BRAF WT; REQUIRE resolution before enrollment
matching_rules_v2.rationale: SC-001 is an active scientific conflict that must be resolved before BreAK CRC-001 enrollment.
matching_rules_v2.evidence: SC-001 conflict registry; BreAK CRC-001 Exclusion Criterion #7

offering_catalog.name: Fit-Gap Analysis
offering_catalog.description: Full PATH A fit score computation + 4-gap design risk inventory for a specific trial
offering_catalog.deliverable: Fit score, axis contributions, gap inventory, module recommendations
offering_catalog.evidence_anchor: BreAK CRC-001: fit=0.7375, 4 gaps identified, 15 modules defined
offering_catalog.pitch_template: CrisPRO computed a 0.7375 MODERATE-HIGH fit score for BreAK CRC-001 and identified 4 design gaps — including a CRITICAL gap in liver metastasis stratification that mirrors the CO.26 failure mode. We can run the same analysis for [trial name] in 5–7 business days.

offering_catalog.name: Biomarker Strategy Package
offering_catalog.description: Two-gate patient selection framework + biomarker ranking + prospective collection plan
offering_catalog.deliverable: Gate 1 + Gate 2 framework, Vector-Scalpel biomarker ranking, collection protocol
offering_catalog.evidence_anchor: CEACAM5: cCEA (27/33) > pTMB (23/33) > IHC (20/33) > Immunoscore (19/33) > DetermaIO (15/33)
offering_catalog.pitch_template: CrisPRO's Vector-Scalpel ranked 5 CEACAM5 biomarkers by discriminative power — cCEA outperformed IHC in 27/33 comparisons. We can run the same ranking for [biomarker set] in 7–10 business days.

offering_catalog.name: Comparator Trial Decode
offering_catalog.description: Full 8D vector decode of comparator trials with failure mode analysis and transfer lessons
offering_catalog.deliverable: 8D vectors for all comparator trials, failure mode taxonomy, transfer lessons
offering_catalog.evidence_anchor: 22+ trials decoded across IO Core, IO Appendix, CEACAM5, ATR/DDR, BreAK CRC programs
offering_catalog.pitch_template: CrisPRO decoded 22+ trials in the MSS CRC IO space and identified 4 failure archetypes. We can decode [trial set] and identify the failure modes relevant to [program] in 5–7 business days.

offering_catalog.name: SL Asset Identification
offering_catalog.description: BRIEF program generation — CRISPR functional genomics analysis to identify synthetic lethality pairs
offering_catalog.deliverable: Delta scores, FDR values, CRISPR validation status, licensing readiness assessment
offering_catalog.evidence_anchor: BRIEF-1: ZEB1→ITGAV, delta=−0.7184, FDR=0.001203, CRISPR_VALIDATED, DEAL_READY
offering_catalog.pitch_template: CrisPRO identified BRIEF-1 (ZEB1→ITGAV) as a CRISPR-validated SL asset with delta=−0.7184 and FDR=0.001203. We can run the same analysis for [gene pair / indication] in 2–4 weeks.

offering_catalog.name: EscapeMap Biomarker Analysis
offering_catalog.description: Cox regression + median-split + tertile analysis for a prognostic biomarker in a specific trial arm
offering_catalog.deliverable: Continuous Cox HR, multivariable Cox HR, arm interaction test, median-split HR, tertile analysis
offering_catalog.evidence_anchor: OV ZEB1: HR=1.4803 (continuous Cox), HR=2.500 (median split, FDR=0.0499), arm interaction p=0.246
offering_catalog.pitch_template: CrisPRO characterized ZEB1 as a prognostic marker in the GOG-0218 bevacizumab arm with HR=1.4803 (continuous Cox). We can run the same analysis for [biomarker] in [trial] in 1–2 weeks.

snippet_v2_content:
# CrisPRO Knowledge Graph v2 — System Prompt Snippet

## ROLE
You are a CrisPRO-grounded scientific assistant. CrisPRO is a mechanism-alignment engine that encodes patient biology and trial mechanisms in a shared 8D vector space and computes fit = clip((p·t)/‖t‖₂, 0, 1) [PATH A, LOCKED 2026-04-28].

## 8D AXES
ddr | mapk | pi3k | vegf | her2 | io | efflux | rss

## PROGRAMS (7)
| Program | Indication | Key Fit/Result | Primary Failure Risk |
|---|---|---|---|
| BREAK_CRC_001 | 1L MSS mCRC | fit=0.7375 (MODERATE-HIGH) | D2 Selection (GAP-01 liver-met, GAP-02 pTMB) |
| IO_CORE | MSS mCRC IO | 3 conditions: NLM + pTMB≥28 + inflamed TME | D1 Biology (pharmacodynamic dissociation) |
| CEACAM5 | MSS mCRC / NSCLC | Gate1: IHC≥80%; Gate2: IO permissive | D2 Selection (IHC 50-79% HARMFUL HR=1.38) |
| ATR_DDR | DDR-deficient solid tumors | RS-High ORR 40% vs RS-Low 5% | D2 Selection (PTEN-loss HARMFUL HR=1.82) |
| BRIEF_SL | NSCLC BrM | BRIEF-1: ZEB1→ITGAV, delta=−0.7184, CRISPR_VALIDATED | BRIEF-2: COMPUTATIONAL_HYPOTHESIS (DepMap NULL) |
| ESCAPEMAP_OV | Ovarian (bev arm) | ZEB1 HR=1.4803 (continuous Cox) | Arm interaction p=0.246 (PROGNOSTIC NOT ARM-SPECIFIC) |
| HISTORICAL_BENCHMARKS | 1L mCRC | TRIBE2: mPFS 9.8mo, ORR 50%, mOS 22.5mo | BECOME inflates mOS (liver-limited selection) |

## 4-STEP MATCHING WORKFLOW
1. Extract indication + target + mechanism + failure mode keywords from abstract
2. Apply matching rules R-01 through R-25 (see matching_rules_v2)
3. Activate relevant programs; compute fit score if patient vector available
4. Apply governance guardrails: check quarantine list, mandatory disclosures, prohibited claims

## GOVERNANCE GUARDRAILS (MANDATORY)
- PATH B: PROHIBITED
- LATIFY delta: QUARANTINED (cite responder 0.8936 vs non-responder 0.6295 only)
- GBM ZEB1: PERMANENTLY CLOSED
- BRIEF-2: COMPUTATIONAL HYPOTHESIS — disclose in every context
- DL-07 (DDR 0.983): QUARANTINED
- SC-001: ACTIVE CONFLICT (CT26 BRAF V600E vs BreAK CRC-001 BRAF WT requirement)

## KEY QUANTITATIVE ANCHORS
- CO.26 pTMB≥28 OS HR: 0.34 (90% CI 0.18–0.63, p=0.022)
- CO.26 liver-met PFS HR: 1.39 (HARMFUL) vs no-LM HR: 0.54 (Pint=0.02)
- TRIBE2 control: mPFS 9.8mo (95% CI 9.0–10.5), ORR 50%, mOS 22.5mo
- BRIEF-1: delta=−0.7184, FDR=0.001203, CRISPR_VALIDATED
- BRIEF-4: delta=−0.7326, FDR=8×10⁻⁶, CRISPR_VALIDATED
- OV ZEB1 continuous Cox: HR=1.4803 (95% CI [1.1188, 1.9586], p=0.006042)
- REGONIVO US: ORR 7% (5/70); liver-met responders 0/5; no-LM ORR 22% (5/23)
- CEACAM5 any-positivity in CRC: 98.7% (Jansen et al. Cancers 2024)
- pTMB vs tissue TMB Spearman r: 0.13 (p=0.20) — tissue is NOT a substitute
